description: validate JCL, then submit it; if validation fails, notify on-call
submit a JCL job via zowe
send an email notification
validate JCL with the local AI model
ask Claude to summarize the latest spool output
dsl:
flow "Summarize Spool" v1.0.0

summarize[cloud_ai: "Summarize spool"] {
  provider: "claude"
  modelId: "claude-3-5-sonnet-latest"
  prompt: "Summarize the spool transcript"
  maxTokens: 512
}

description: validate JCL, then submit it
validate the JCL, submit it, then archive the spool to s3
submit the job; on success, archive the output, on failure, escalate to a human
use zosmf to retrieve job status
ssh into the build host and run the deploy script
run a shell command to print the working directory
check git status of the working tree
use OpenAI to draft a release note from the latest commit message
dsl:
flow "Draft Release Note" v1.0.0

draft[cloud_ai: "Draft note"] {
  provider: "openai"
  modelId: "gpt-4o-mini"
  prompt: "Draft a release note from the commit message"
  maxTokens: 256
  temperature: 0.2
}

description: ask Gemini to triage the build failure log
dsl:
flow "Triage Build Failure" v1.0.0

triage[cloud_ai: "Triage failure"] {
  provider: "gemini"
  modelId: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  prompt: "Triage the build failure log"
  maxTokens: 1024
}

description: run a mock action for testing the canvas
validate the JCL; on success, submit it and then archive; on failure, send an email and stop
always run cleanup after the deploy regardless of result
validate JCL with strict thresholds: 0.95 for high confidence, 0.7 for low
validate the JCL with the local model; if it passes, submit via zowe and then archive the spool; if it fails, ask Claude to explain why
send an email and then create a JIRA ticket
run npm install in the apps/desktop folder
dsl:
flow "npm install" v1.0.0

install[action: "npm install"] {
  adapter: "shell"
  actionId: "npm"
  args: "install"
  cwd: "apps/desktop"
}

description: curl a health endpoint
use Claude to draft a postmortem and persist the prompt and response in execution history for audit
dsl:
flow "Draft Postmortem" v1.0.0

draft[cloud_ai: "Draft postmortem"] {
  provider: "claude"
  modelId: "claude-3-5-sonnet-latest"
  prompt: "Draft a postmortem from the incident timeline"
  maxTokens: 1024
  auditContent: true
}

description: submit a JCL job using the staging zowe connection
after submitting the job, both archive to s3 and send a notification email
validate JCL and check disk space; only when both pass, submit the job
use Gemini to translate a release note to German
dsl:
flow "Translate Note" v1.0.0

translate[cloud_ai: "Translate"] {
  provider: "gemini"
  modelId: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  prompt: "Translate the release note to German"
  maxTokens: 512
}

description: run cargo test across the workspace
list pods on the staging cluster
validate the JCL locally, submit it via zowe, archive the spool to s3, and on validation failure ask Claude to explain and email the explanation to on-call
submit a JCL deck named payload.jcl using the generic zowe cli-raw action
submit a JCL deck via zowe cli-raw and capture the jobid with a regex output filter
dsl:
flow "Submit and Capture" v1.0.0

submit[action: "Submit JCL"] {
  adapter: "zowe"
  actionId: "cli-raw"
  command: "jobs submit local-file ./payload.jcl"
  outputFilter: "JOB(\\d+)"
}

description: download a partitioned data set member named SYS1.RAI.SOURCE(DMCLRAI) using zowe files download data-set
issue a DCMT VARY DMCL NEW COPY operator command on the production CV via zowe console issue command
dsl:
flow "Activate New DMCL" v1.0.0

activate[action: "Activate DMCL"] {
  adapter: "zowe"
  actionId: "cli-raw"
  command: "console issue command F idmscv,DCMT VARY DMCL NEW COPY"
  connectionId: "prod-zowe"
}

description: in parallel, print the live DMCL load module via a print job and download the source DDL from a PDS, then ask Claude to diff them and flag any drift

provision the Smart Restart IDMS sync database end to end: allocate the VSAM file, submit the DMCL DDL, submit the DBTABLE DDL, submit the schema and subschema DDL, format the file in local mode, then issue DCMT VARY DMCL NEW COPY
pre-promotion gate for a new DMCL: build it in staging, run the Smart Restart IVPs, validate the structure with the local AI model, and on pass issue VARY DMCL NEW COPY in production
submit a JCL job and download its spool by piping the captured jobid into the next zowe command using the dollar-IN placeholder
submit a JCL job restart-aware: submit, capture jobid, poll status, on pass archive, on fail download spool, ask Claude to interpret the failure, and resubmit with restart cards
before submitting the JCL, run a shell command to confirm the local file exists, then submit via zowe and archive on success
list failed restart-enabled jobs older than seven days, ask Claude which to delete versus restart, then submit the SRSUDEL utility for each delete decision
check whether the Smart Restart IDMS sync VSAM file has headroom: list active restart-enabled jobs and run an IDCAMS LISTCAT, then ask Claude to compute pass or fail with margin
install dependencies, build the project, then run the test suite
run the test suite; if it passes, deploy; if it fails, post to Slack
run linting and unit tests in parallel, then commit if both pass
fetch the artefact; on failure, retry the same node
dsl:
flow "fetch-with-retry" v1

fetch[action: "Fetch artefact"] {
  adapter: "shell"
  actionId: "curl"
  args: "-fsSL https://example.com/artefact.tar.gz -o /tmp/a.tar.gz"
}

fetch.fail --> fetch

description: build the project; if it fails, ask the local model to suggest a fix
run the test suite; on failure, send the output to Claude for triage
have the local model interpret the build log, log a one-line summary, then run the suggested fix command
list jobs on the test LPAR using the saved connection
dsl:
flow "list-jobs" v1

list[action: "List jobs"] {
  adapter: "zowe"
  actionId: "cli-raw"
  command: "jobs list"
  connectionId: "lpar-test"
}

description: list the spool files for a job, then unzip the result locally
ask Claude to draft a release-note bullet for the latest commit
dsl:
flow "release-note-draft" v1

draft[cloud_ai: "Draft note"] {
  provider: "claude"
  modelId: "claude-opus-4-5"
  prompt: "Write a one-sentence release-note bullet from the most recent commit message."
  maxTokens: 256
}

description: submit a job, wait, then run a shell command that fetches the result
just make a node that does nothing for five seconds
format the codebase, then run rustfmt check; on fail, run cargo fmt and re-check
run typescript type-check; only on success run the build
run a smoke curl, then a deeper integration curl, then a slow soak
verify the deployment then on failure scale up replicas
list pods then on success describe the first one for diagnostics
fetch payload from two mirrors in parallel; merge once both finish
build the rust workspace and the node workspace in parallel; publish only if both pass
run cargo test and cargo clippy together; on either failing, log
ping the staging endpoint; retry up to a configured limit on fail
dsl:
flow "ping-retry" v1

ping[action: "Healthcheck"] {
  adapter: "shell"
  actionId: "curl"
  args: "-fsS https://staging.example.com/health"
}

ping.fail --> ping

description: submit jcl; on submission failure retry the same submission
dsl:
flow "submit-retry" v1

submit[action: "Submit JCL"] {
  adapter: "zowe"
  actionId: "cli-raw"
  command: "jobs submit local-file"
  connectionId: "lpar-test"
}

submit.fail --> submit

description: run integration tests; on fail, ask Gemini to summarise stack traces
build the docker image; if it succeeds use OpenAI to suggest a tag name
diff main against the current branch; ask Claude whether to squash
have Claude draft a postmortem outline, then sleep, then archive
list zos jobs; if listing fails, sleep 10 seconds and retry
submit a JCL job and have the local interpreter explain the result
list datasets; on success branch on payload, on fail log the error
wait one second, log a heartbeat, wait again
log a starting line then sleep then log a finishing line
branch on previous status; both branches converge on a single final step
run a build, ask the local model to summarise output, then post the summary
run npm audit, then ssh to the bastion to log the result
run a kubectl apply; on fail, run kubectl describe to dump diagnostics
have OpenAI generate a SQL migration name
ask Gemini to label severity for a given log line
interpret a recent spool dump
view JCL for a job and grep for a particular DD statement
run a security scan, ask Claude to merge findings, post to Slack
have Claude triage a ticket; on a triage decision branch, run a curl against the relevant API
interpret then re-interpret with cloud reinforcement
fetch, validate, transform, and post a payload in four steps
lint, typecheck, test, build, deploy in a five-stage chain
minimal one-step shell echo
single git status
single zowe list-datasets
list restart-enabled jobs older than 7 days then have Claude classify which to delete
submit JCL via zowe; on success, immediately run the local spool interpreter on the result
before submit, validate the JCL filename via shell, then submit
